item.title: AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-11 - Today's Summary
item.description: The dominant pattern today is the sudden breakout of 'addyosmani/agent-skills', which gained 2,051 stars in 24 hours with 4x acceleration, reflecting intense developer interest in structured, reusable skill packs for AI coding agents. This is reinforced by the sustained hot streak of 'x1xhlol/system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools' (139k stars, 2.8x acceleration) and the continued growth of 'nexu-io/open-design' and 'rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch', both of which package expert knowledge into agent-ready formats. Meanwhile, the MCP/tool-calling category shows strong weekly momentum (avg 2,673 stars/week) but no breakout today, suggesting steady adoption rather than a spike. The absence of new product launches on Product Hunt and the Hacker News focus on Claude Fable 5 (2,547 votes) indicate that developer attention is split between practical agent tooling and frontier model releases. Looking forward, the convergence of skill-pack repositories and system-prompt collections may indicate a maturing ecosystem where the bottleneck shifts from agent capability to expert knowledge encoding. Top projects: addyosmani/agent-skills, esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix, x1xhlol/system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools, nexu-io/open-design, rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch, tirth8205/code-review-graph, affaan-m/ECC, santifer/career-ops, HKUDS/nanobot, sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills, rtk-ai/rtk, getagentseal/codeburn Note: GitHub API 只提供当前 stars，总量变化来自本项目保存的历史 snapshot。 Note: daily delta 使用约 24h 前 snapshot，weekly delta 使用约 7 天前 snapshot。 Note: Accelerating 只展示至少 3 天历史基线且今日 delta 超过前 3 日均值 2 倍的项目。 Note: Potential Score 为规则评分，用于排序，不代表项目质量结论。

item.title: [sudden_breakout] addyosmani/agent-skills (+2051 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: A production-grade pack of 24 structured skills and slash commands that encode senior engineering workflows for AI coding agents. Why it matters: The repo has exploded from 0 to 51,894 stars with a 24h delta of 2,051 and a 7d delta of 3,844. The acceleration ratio is 4 (high), indicating a sudden breakout. This is not gradual growth — it's a viral spike driven by the release of a comprehensive, well-documented skill pack for multiple agent platforms. Developer insight: The project signals a shift from ad-hoc agent prompting to structured, skill-based agent programming. It suggests that the future of AI-assisted development lies in reusable, verifiable skill packs rather than raw LLM prompts. Developers should consider adopting or contributing to such skill ecosystems. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix (+492 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent Description: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go with prefix-cache stability for low-cost long sessions. Why it matters: With 20,849 stars, a 24-hour delta of +492, and a 7-day delta of +3,475, the project shows sustained high velocity. The acceleration ratio of 0.7 indicates steady, organic growth rather than a sudden spike, making it a reliable signal of ongoing community interest. Developer insight: For AI developers, this signals a shift toward specialized, model-native agents that optimize for specific provider strengths (e.g., DeepSeek's cache). It also highlights the value of config-driven, plugin-extensible architectures for building versatile coding tools. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] x1xhlol/system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools (+360 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent Description: A massive, community-curated collection of system prompts, internal tools, and AI models from dozens of popular AI coding assistants and platforms. Why it matters: The repo has exploded to 139k stars with a 24h delta of 278 and a 7d delta of 549, showing sustained high velocity. The acceleration ratio of 6.1 indicates a sudden breakout, likely driven by viral sharing across developer communities and social media. The star history chart shows a hockey-stick growth pattern, making it one of the fastest-growing AI repos on GitHub. Developer insight: The massive interest signals a strong developer appetite for transparency and customization in AI tools. Expect more open-source efforts to replicate or improve upon proprietary system prompts. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] nexu-io/open-design (+569 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent Description: Local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that turns your coding agent into a design engine with 150+ design systems, 260+ plugins, and multi-format export. Why it matters: With 62,959 stars, a 24-hour delta of +569, and a 7-day average of +959 stars per day, Open Design is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 0.5 indicates steady momentum rather than a sudden spike. The recent 0.9.0 release (within 30 days) and active commits within the last 7 days show the project is actively maintained and improving. This is a project that has already captured significant developer mindshare and continues to grow. Developer insight: The shift from pixel-pushing to agent-native design is real. Open Design's architecture — skills, design systems, plugins, and MCP server — is a blueprint for how AI tools can compose with existing developer workflows. The BYOK proxy and support for 21+ CLIs mean it's not locked to any one model provider, which is a key adoption driver. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch (+294 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production-grade agents, shipping a reusable artifact per lesson. Why it matters: With 31,049 stars, a 7-day delta of 3,156 (avg 1,931/day), and a 24h delta of 294, this repo is experiencing sustained explosive growth. The acceleration of 0.4 indicates high but stable velocity, not a sudden spike. The project has 150,639 readers and 241,669 page views in the last 30 days, signaling massive community adoption. It's trending because it fills a clear gap: practical, deep AI education that produces real artifacts. Developer insight: The market is shifting from API-wrapping to deep understanding. Developers who can build from scratch and ship production artifacts will have a significant advantage. This repo signals that the community values rigorous, hands-on learning over quick demos. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] tirth8205/code-review-graph (+46 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: MCP / Tool Calling Description: Local-first code intelligence graph that slashes AI token usage by ~82x median during code reviews via MCP and CLI. Why it matters: With 18,337 stars, a 24h delta of +46, and a 7-day average of +76.2 stars/day, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.6 (moderate) indicates steady organic interest rather than a viral spike, making it a reliable tool for the AI coding ecosystem. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward local-first, context-aware AI tooling that minimizes API costs and latency. Developers building AI coding agents should consider integrating similar graph-based context pruning to stay competitive. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] affaan-m/ECC (+835 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: MCP / Tool Calling Description: A harness-native operator system that optimizes AI agent performance across multiple coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) with skills, memory, security, and continuous learning. Why it matters: With 212,749 stars and a sustained 7-day average of 1,604 new stars per day, ECC is one of the fastest-growing developer tools on GitHub. The 24-hour delta of 835 stars and acceleration of 0.6 (high) indicate continued strong organic interest. The recent v2.0.0 release (June 2026) with 261 skills, operator workflows, and a Rust control-plane alpha likely fueled the latest surge. Developer insight: The rise of ECC signals a maturing AI developer tool ecosystem where users demand consistent, optimized experiences across multiple agents. This trend suggests that standalone agent configurations are insufficient; developers want integrated systems that manage the entire agent lifecycle. The opportunity lies in building complementary tools or plugins that integrate with ECC's skill and hook system. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] santifer/career-ops (+793 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Workflow Automation Description: Career-Ops turns any AI coding CLI into a job search command center, automating offer evaluation, CV tailoring, and portal scanning. Why it matters: With 52,503 stars, a 24-hour delta of 793, and a 7-day average of 662.8 stars per day, Career-Ops is experiencing sustained explosive growth. The acceleration of 0.8 indicates steady, high-velocity attention, not a sudden spike. It's trending because it directly addresses the pain of modern job hunting with a practical, AI-powered tool that has proven results (740+ evaluations, 100+ CVs, one dream role landed). Developer insight: Career-Ops signals a shift toward AI-powered personal productivity tools that leverage coding CLIs as general-purpose agent runtimes. Developers should watch for more 'agentic CLI' patterns that combine LLM reasoning with browser automation and local file generation. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] HKUDS/nanobot (+50 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: An ultra-lightweight, open-source personal AI agent that you can truly own, with WebUI, chat channels, tools, memory, MCP, model routing, and automation. Why it matters: With 44,009 stars, a 7-day average delta of 84 stars/day, and a high acceleration of 0.6, this project is experiencing sustained, organic growth. The recent v0.2.1 release (The Workbench Release) on 2026-06-01 adds a packaged WebUI, project workspaces, and broader provider/channel support, fueling continued attention. Developer insight: The trend toward lightweight, self-hosted AI agents is accelerating. This project shows that the community values simplicity, extensibility, and multi-platform support over monolithic solutions. Developers should consider adopting or contributing to such frameworks to stay ahead. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills (+111 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: MCP / Tool Calling Description: An installable library of 1,527+ reusable SKILL.md playbooks for AI coding assistants like Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini CLI. Why it matters: With 40,292 stars, a 24h delta of +111, and a 7-day average of +136.6 stars/day, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 0.8 indicates steady, organic attention rather than a sudden spike. It's clearly resonating with the AI coding assistant community. Developer insight: The project signals a shift from ad-hoc prompting to structured, reusable skill libraries. Developers should expect more such curated collections as AI assistants become central to daily workflows. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] rtk-ai/rtk (+429 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: MCP / Tool Calling Description: CLI proxy that reduces LLM token consumption by 60-90% on common dev commands. Single Rust binary, zero dependencies Why it matters: MCP / Tool Calling 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +429，总 stars 61,058，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。 Developer insight: 重点看协议适配、工具注册和真实示例，判断它是否能降低 agent 接工具的成本。 Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] getagentseal/codeburn (+42 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: See where your AI coding tokens go. Interactive TUI dashboard for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor cost observability. Why it matters: AI Agent Framework 方向一周增长 +262，属于持续升温信号。 Developer insight: 可以用一个最小样例验证 API、文档和集成成本，再决定是否纳入长期技术观察。 Risk: Low
